name: Lu Lens Beginners Pie Crust
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the flour and salt together in a bowl.
2. Cut in the shortening with a pastry blender or fork until the shortening is the size of green peas.
3. Loosely separate the flour-fat mixture into three portions.
4. Add ice water, a little at a time, to one portion, tossing the mixture with the pastry blender or fork until it is moist and holds together. Set that section aside.
5. Repeat steps 3 and 4 with the other two portions.
6. When all the portions are moistened, gather them into a ball and flatten into a round disc. Wrap with plastic wrap and chill for at least half an hour.
7. Flour a board or wax paper and, using a floured or stockinged rolling pin, roll the pastry into a round about 1/8 inch thick and at least 1 1/2 to 2 inches larger than your pan.
8. Fold in quarters. Place the pastry in a 9-inch pie pan and unfold.
9. Trim the pastry 1 inch larger than the pie pan and tuck the overhanging pastry under itself.
10. Use the thumb and index finger of one hand to flute the edges.
11. Place in the freezer or refrigerator for 30 minutes before baking.
12. To prebake, preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit. Crumple a piece of wax paper, then spread it out to the edges of the pan. Fill with raw rice or dried peas to make a weight.
13. Bake for 20 minutes.
14. Carefully remove the paper and filling from the pie crust.
15. Fill the crust with your chosen filling and bake according to filling directions. If the filling requires no cooking, bake the pie shell an additional 10 minutes.
Yield: 1 9-inch pie shell
